Yes — I pulled the latest values from `helios_dosing_params_v3.yaml`.

**Quick dosing summary for Dr. Martinez**

| Patient | Age | Weight | Dose | Calculated dose | Frequency | Next dose | PK clearance | Notes |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---|---|---:|---|
| CU-001 | 34 mo | 12.4 kg | 0.15 mg/kg | **1.86 mg** | q3w | 2026-04-14 | 3.2 mL/min | Stable; 22% tumor regression at last scan |
| CU-002 | 18 mo | 9.1 kg | 0.12 mg/kg | **1.09 mg** | q3w | 2026-04-12 | 2.8 mL/min | Responding well; weight gain improving |
| CU-003 | 51 mo | 15.7 kg | 0.18 mg/kg | **2.83 mg** | q2w | 2026-04-10 | 4.1 mL/min | New enrollment; first dose given 2026-03-27 |

**PK model status**
From the dosing config alone, nothing jumps out as unstable or internally inconsistent.  
- **CU-001 / CU-002:** look clinically stable or improving based on notes  
- **CU-003:** early in treatment, so closer follow-up is expected

That said, a dosing config snapshot **isn’t enough to fully verify PK model stability**. To confirm that, I’d need the latest monitoring or model-validation outputs rather than just the parameter file.

If you want, I can also format this into a one-paragraph handoff note for Dr. Martinez.
